project_hint.name: Personal Website Frontend
project_hint.description: Vue 3 + Vite + TypeScript personal portfolio SPA with typed content modules, section-composed homepage, and GitHub Pages deployment
project_hint.role: Static personal website showcasing career stories, projects, and resume

technologies_found.name: Vue 3
technologies_found.role_in_project: Renders the SPA with Composition API script-setup SFCs; HomePage composes section components fed by typed content props
technologies_found.typical_purpose: Progressive JavaScript framework for building reactive user interfaces
technologies_found.advantages_over_alternatives: Lightweight runtime for a content-heavy static site; script setup keeps section components concise without a global state library

technologies_found.name: Vite
technologies_found.role_in_project: Dev server, production bundler, and @ alias resolver mapping @ to src for imports across pages and content modules
technologies_found.typical_purpose: Fast frontend build tool with native ESM dev server and Rollup-based production builds
technologies_found.advantages_over_alternatives: Near-instant HMR for iterative content and layout work; vue-tsc + vite build script enforces type-check before static output

technologies_found.name: TypeScript
technologies_found.role_in_project: Defines HomePageContent, ProjectEntry, and related interfaces so homepage and project pages share a typed content contract
technologies_found.typical_purpose: Static typing for JavaScript applications to catch schema drift at compile time
technologies_found.advantages_over_alternatives: Content modules import types from a single contract file; vue-tsc blocks builds when section props or content shapes diverge

technologies_found.name: Vue Router
technologies_found.role_in_project: SPA routing for home, project detail (slug param), about, resume, and contact with lazy-loaded page components and scroll-to-top behavior
technologies_found.typical_purpose: Official client-side router for Vue.js single-page applications
technologies_found.advantages_over_alternatives: Code-split page bundles via dynamic import(); createWebHistory suits GitHub Pages hosting behind a static dist output

technologies_found.name: CSS custom properties
technologies_found.role_in_project: Centralizes dark-theme palette, typography scale, spacing, and accent colors consumed by layout and section component styles
technologies_found.typical_purpose: Design tokens as CSS variables for consistent theming without a CSS preprocessor
technologies_found.advantages_over_alternatives: No Tailwind or Sass dependency; layered @import chain (tokens → base → patterns → layout → section-components) keeps styling maintainable for a small site

technologies_found.name: GitHub Actions
technologies_found.role_in_project: CI pipeline on master push: npm ci, vue-tsc + vite build, upload dist artifact, deploy to GitHub Pages with custom domain therealdom.com
technologies_found.typical_purpose: Hosted CI/CD for building and deploying repositories
technologies_found.advantages_over_alternatives: Free static hosting integrated with the repo; no separate CDN or server config beyond Pages permissions and CNAME

patterns_found.name: Content-as-data modules
patterns_found.description: Homepage, projects, and about copy live in src/content/*.ts as typed exports rather than hard-coded in templates
patterns_found.role_in_project: Separates editorial content from presentation so copy updates do not require touching Vue markup
patterns_found.advantages_over_alternatives: TypeScript interfaces enforce section shape; easier to diff content changes than scattered template strings

patterns_found.name: Section composition
patterns_found.description: HomePage imports discrete section SFCs (HeroSection, TimelineSection, etc.) and passes slices of homeContent as props
patterns_found.role_in_project: Maps each homepage anchor (stories, career, stack, about) to an isolated component with a narrow prop contract
patterns_found.advantages_over_alternatives: Each section can evolve independently; jump-nav hrefs align with section IDs without a monolithic homepage template

patterns_found.name: Lazy route loading
patterns_found.description: Router routes use () => import('@/pages/...') for all page components
patterns_found.role_in_project: Splits project detail and secondary pages into separate chunks loaded on navigation
patterns_found.advantages_over_alternatives: Smaller initial bundle for the homepage-first visitor path; standard Vue Router pattern without extra lazy-load library

patterns_found.name: Layout shell
patterns_found.description: App.vue renders PageShell which owns site chrome (header nav, mobile menu) and RouterView for page content
patterns_found.role_in_project: Keeps navigation and accessibility scaffolding in one layout while pages focus on section content
patterns_found.advantages_over_alternatives: Consistent nav and mobile toggle behavior across routes without duplicating header markup per page

accomplishments_found.title: Typed content-driven portfolio SPA
accomplishments_found.role_in_project: Personal portfolio SPA where homepage sections and project case studies are driven by typed content modules and composed section components
accomplishments_found.advantages_over_alternatives: Editorial updates stay in TypeScript content files with compile-time validation instead of a CMS or markdown pipeline for a single-author site

accomplishments_found.title: GitHub Pages static deployment
accomplishments_found.role_in_project: Automated build-and-deploy pipeline producing static dist output to GitHub Pages with custom domain CNAME
accomplishments_found.advantages_over_alternatives: Zero server maintenance; push-to-master triggers type-checked production build and Pages deploy

architecture_notes: Static SPA: Vite builds to dist/; GitHub Pages serves index.html and assets with createWebHistory routing (no SSR).
Data flow: src/content/*.ts exports → page/section components via typed props → scoped or global CSS from tokens.css layer chain.
Project detail pages resolve slugs via getProjectBySlug in projects.ts and render fullStory blocks split by paragraph.
Resume PDF served from public/DamanveerSingh_Resume.pdf; portable Node under .tools/ optional for Windows environments without system npm.
